mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately depress equity valuations over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion, which drives corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output that translates into higher corporate profit margins, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days specifically during early-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases produce negative or flat 20-day forward returns during early-cycle expansions, with a mean return difference not exceeding 0.5% over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly correlates with aggregate corporate revenue expansion, creating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 prices over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show zero or negative correlation with GDPC1 growth rates across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL breaches the 0.4% monthly threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which boosts consumer spending forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to generate positive 20-day forward returns with a hit rate above 55% in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales during late-cycle expansions signal peak consumer exhaustion, which rapidly erodes equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS declines during late-cycle expansions are followed by positive 5-day forward returns in > 65% of observed cases, invalidating the peak-demand reversal mechanism.
